campus: Genoa (Villa Cambiaso, Polo Opera Pia, Polo Economia)

academic_requirements.summary: Open-access (free) bachelor's programme delivered entirely in English. Enrolment requires a high-school diploma (or a recognised equivalent foreign qualification). The programme is structured around IMO-STCW competences (Deck Officer and Marine Engineer/Electrotechnical Officer curricula). No selection ranking and no aptitude entry test are required.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A high-school diploma valid for university admission, or a foreign qualification recognised as equivalent.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: No specific subject prerequisites are published; the programme embraces IMO-STCW requirements (Convention sections A-II/1, A-II/2, A-III/1, A-III/2, A-III/6).
academic_requirements.ofa_rule: An initial-preparation verification is part of the enrolment procedure; Maritime Science & Technology enrollees do NOT need to take the aptitude test (AT).
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Non-EU candidates residing abroad with a foreign diploma apply via UniGeApply for pre-evaluation before enrolment.

accepted_proofs: A B2-or-higher English certificate among those accepted by the University, listed on the CLAT certifications page (https://clat.unige.it/CertificazioniRiconosciute).
exemption_rules: Holding a verified B2-or-higher certification allows the B2 level to be recognised and exempts the candidate from the CLAT English assessment test.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: Candidates without a B2-or-higher certificate must take the University's CLAT English assessment test at B2 level. Teaching is entirely in English ('the official language of the maritime sector').

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: CLAT English assessment test (B2) only for candidates without an accepted B2 certificate. No aptitude/selection entry test.
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Applicants lacking a B2-or-higher English certificate take the CLAT B2 English test; there is no aptitude AT for this programme.
entry_exam_or_test.format: English language assessment at B2 level via CLAT.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: [uncertain] Specific CLAT session dates not captured from the official page.

rounds: Spring session for EU citizens and non-EU residents in Italy (UniGeApply): opens 9 April 2026; bachelor's degrees close 25 September 2026.
First-year enrolment confirmation and fee payment follow the UniGe enrolment calendar after registration/pre-evaluation.
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Enrolment in years after the first runs from 15 July to 5 October 2026, with late payment (arrears) accepted from 6 October to 5 November 2026.
application_deadline_eu.notes: Open-access programme; EU/resident applicants are not ranked. Non-EU candidates residing abroad must use the fall-session UniGeApply route below.

rounds: Fall session for non-EU candidates requiring a visa (UniGeApply): opens 26 November 2025; institution-wide close 20 March 2026; pre-evaluation results by 22 May 2026.
Programme-specific early closure: Maritime Science and Technology (bachelor, English) closed in the fall session on 18 February 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: After UniGeApply eligibility, visa applicants complete Universitaly pre-enrolment (Universitaly opened 4 May 2026; University validation from 18 May 2026) and then apply for the study visa.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: The early-closure date (18 Feb 2026) is published on the UniGeApply page and matches the project's known_context. The UniGeApply fall window for A.Y. 2026/27 has closed.

required_documents.notes: Passport mandatory for non-EU applicants (jpg on UniGeApply); EU applicants may use an ID card.
Uploaded as PDF (max 2 MB) on UniGeApply for pre-evaluation.

required_documents.document_name: Legalization of the qualification or CIMEA Statement of Verification, plus Declaration of Value or CIMEA Statement of Comparability
required_documents.notes: Required for non-EU visa applicants with a foreign qualification to complete enrolment.

required_documents.document_name: English language proficiency certificate (B2) or proof of English-medium instruction
required_documents.notes: Candidates without an accepted B2 certificate must take the CLAT B2 English test.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment submission and study-visa evidence
required_documents.notes: Visa applicants complete Universitaly pre-enrolment after UniGeApply eligibility, then apply for the study visa.

source_quotes.quote: MARITIME SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY (course code 11929), CORSO DI LAUREA TRIENNALE, Degree Class L-28 R (NAVIGATION), 180 CFU
Teaching is totally in English, the official language of the maritime sector.
This is an open access degree course. In order to enrol, you must have a high school diploma (or other foreign qualification recognised as equivalent).
Maritime Science & Technology enrollees do not need to take the AT
If you do not have a B2 or higher (C1 or C2) language certification, you must take an English language test at the B2 level.
Maritime Science and Technology (bachelor's, English): early closure 18 February 2026 (fall session)

uncertainty_notes: Physical/health (STCW medical fitness / embarkation) requirements: the official admission-criteria page does NOT state a medical-fitness certificate as an admission condition. The programme is built on IMO-STCW competences (A-II and A-III sections) for officers, but any medical fitness certificate for shipboard training/embarkation is not published as an enrolment requirement on the pages reviewed; confirm with DITEN (didatticaditen@unige.it).
Restricted-access selection: confirmed the programme is open access, NOT restricted; no selection ranking and no aptitude test (AT).
CLAT English test specific session dates were not captured from the official page.
EU/spring deadlines reflect institution-wide UniGeApply windows; the corsi.unige.it deadlines page lists only the generic enrolment calendar.
